subject: Public Health
title: Malaria's Ongoing Toll
description: A fast breakdown of Malaria's Ongoing Toll for builders.

hook.text: A child in Africa dies of malaria every minute—608,000 deaths per year, mostly children under five.

buildup.text: Plasmodium parasites, spread by Anopheles mosquitoes, destroy red blood cells and overwhelm young immune systems.

discovery.text: Insecticide-treated bed nets reduce malaria deaths by 50%—one of the most cost-effective interventions known.

twist.text: Mosquito resistance to insecticides and parasite resistance to drugs are both growing—threatening decades of progress.

climax.text: New tools—RTS,S and R21 vaccines, gene-drive mosquitoes, and new drugs—are arriving but need massive investment.

punchline.text: A bed net costs $2 and saves a life. Scale is the only barrier.

quiz.question: By how much do insecticide-treated bed nets reduce malaria deaths?
